acceptance_criteria: `cargo test -p handshake-native --test test_theme` passes: all palette token value tests pass (each Color32 field compared to expected hex value).
Syntax token test: dark palette syntax_keyword == Color32::from_rgb(0x56, 0x9c, 0xd6).
Unknown override key is silently ignored (no panic, palette otherwise unchanged).
apply_to_ctx does not panic when called with either light or dark palette on a headless egui::Context.

Running the app (`cargo run -p handshake-native`) and pressing the theme toggle button visually switches the window background between light (#f8fafc) and dark (#121418). Verified by screenshot or kittest pixel comparison.
Theme toggle button has AccessKit NodeId(10) set and Role::Button (verified by kittest or AccessKit tree dump).

No widget in the codebase uses a hardcoded egui::Color32 literal for semantic colors (enforced by a grep check in the test: grep for 'Color32::from_rgb' or 'Color32::WHITE/BLACK' outside of palette.rs and syntax.rs must return zero matches in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/ excluding palette.rs and syntax.rs).
`cargo check -p handshake-native` continues to pass after this MT's changes (MT-002 skeleton remains compilable).
proof_targets: `cargo test -p handshake-native --test test_theme -- --nocapture` exits 0 and prints 'PASS: all palette tokens correct'.
Grep check: `grep -r 'Color32::from_rgb\|Color32::WHITE\|Color32::BLACK' src/frontend/handshake_native/src/ --include='*.rs' | grep -v palette.rs | grep -v syntax.rs` returns zero lines.

Screenshot or kittest snapshot: window background is #121418 (dark) initially; after a synthetic click on the theme toggle button (AccessKit NodeId 10), background changes to #f8fafc (light). If using kittest, compare the background pixel of the central panel before and after the click event.
`cargo test -p handshake-native 2>&1 | grep FAILED` returns empty (all tests pass).
implementation_notes: rgba() parsing: for accent_soft dark mode value 'rgba(34,197,94,0.18)', convert alpha 0.18 to u8 as (0.18 * 255.0).round() as u8 = 46. Store as Color32::from_rgba_premultiplied(34, 197, 94, 46). Note: egui uses premultiplied alpha; for with_overrides() parsing of rgba() strings, apply premultiplication: r_pre = (r * a / 255), g_pre = (g * a / 255), b_pre = (b * a / 255).
HsPalette must be Clone so HandshakeApp can store the current palette independently of the current theme (for override support later).
apply_to_ctx is called every frame to support live token overrides without requiring a restart. This is cheap because egui::Context::set_visuals() does not allocate.
Do not use egui::Style for layout spacing in this MT — only egui::Visuals for colors. Spacing tokens (padding, gap) are a later MT concern.

The theme module is under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/theme/. Add 'mod theme;' to lib.rs (create lib.rs if it does not exist, pointing to the existing main.rs as bin). Or, if the crate remains bin-only, add 'mod theme;' at the top of main.rs or use a src/theme/ path referenced from app.rs directly.

For the font loading prerequisite: add a const INTER_REGULAR: Option<&[u8]> = None; placeholder for now, or use a cfg attribute. The actual include_bytes! path will be set in MT-004 when the assets are bundled. Use an #[cfg(feature = "bundled-fonts")] gate so MT-003 compiles without the font file present.

The HsTheme enum must implement serde::Serialize + serde::Deserialize for future persistence via the workspace settings API (GET/PUT /workspaces/{id}/settings). This is a no-cost forward-compatibility step.

Test file tests/native_gui/test_theme.rs should use #[test] (not egui_kittest) for the pure palette unit tests. The visual switch test can use egui_kittest::Harness if available, or be marked #[ignore] with a note 'requires display; run manually' if headless pixel comparison is not practical.
ports_from_react: app/src/App.css — :root block (lines 1-20): exact light mode token values to port to HsPalette::light().
app/src/App.css — [data-theme='dark'] block (lines 38-52): exact dark mode token values to port to HsPalette::dark().

app/src/lib/workspaceSettings.ts lines 5-12: WorkspaceTheme type ('light'|'dark') and custom_theme_tokens: Record<string,string> — ported as HsTheme enum and the overrides HashMap<String,String> parameter to with_overrides().
app/src/components/SettingsMenu.tsx lines 163-166 (handleThemeChange): React pattern for runtime theme switching — native equivalent is the toggle button in HandshakeApp::update().
binds_backend_api: GET http://127.0.0.1:37501/workspaces/{id}/settings -> WorkspaceSettingsState (includes theme: 'light'|'dark' and custom_theme_tokens). NOT bound in MT-003 (the settings API binding is a later MT). The HsTheme enum and with_overrides() function are designed so the binding can be added without changing the core types.
PUT http://127.0.0.1:37501/workspaces/{id}/settings -> persists theme choice. NOT bound in MT-003.

risks: RISK-1: apply_to_ctx is called every frame (60fps+); egui::Context::set_visuals() under the hood may trigger a full style re-layout, causing jank if it is expensive.

RISK-2: rgba() color strings in the CSS (e.g. accent_soft dark mode) are silently mis-parsed, producing wrong premultiplied alpha values that look correct in isolation but are subtly wrong in compositing.
RISK-3: Custom override map with invalid hex strings panics the app rather than failing gracefully.
RISK-4: Token drift — later MTs hardcode Color32 values because the theme module is not well-documented, causing the no-hardcode invariant to break silently.
RISK-5: The egui Visuals struct fields change between minor egui versions (0.31 -> 0.32+), requiring manual update of apply_to_ctx when the toolkit is upgraded.
RISK-6: Font loading failure at include_bytes! compile time (file path wrong) causes a compile error that blocks all MT-003+ work until the asset is present.
minimum_controls: CONTROL-1 (RISK-1): Cache the last applied HsTheme in HandshakeApp and only call apply_to_ctx when the theme has changed (compare current_theme != last_applied_theme). On no change, skip the call. This reduces set_visuals() to once-per-theme-switch.

CONTROL-2 (RISK-2): Add an explicit unit test for each rgba() token: test that parse_hex_color('rgba(34,197,94,0.18)') == Color32::from_rgba_premultiplied(6, 35, 17, 46) (premultiplied). Document the premultiplication formula in a comment in palette.rs.

CONTROL-3 (RISK-3): with_overrides() must use .unwrap_or(current_value) for every parse attempt — unknown or invalid values silently leave the token unchanged. Add a test: passing an invalid hex string '#ZZZZZZ' must return the original dark palette unchanged.

CONTROL-4 (RISK-4): The test_theme.rs grep check (no hardcoded Color32 outside palette.rs/syntax.rs) is part of the acceptance criteria and must run in CI. Document the convention in a doc comment at the top of theme/mod.rs: '// All colors in handshake-native come from HsPalette. Do not use Color32 literals outside this module.'

CONTROL-5 (RISK-5): Pin egui to an exact version in Cargo.toml ([dependencies] egui = "=0.31.x"). When upgrading, update apply_to_ctx fields to match. Note the exact egui Visuals field list used in apply_to_ctx in a comment block so the upgrade diff is obvious.

CONTROL-6 (RISK-6): Gate the font loading behind #[cfg(feature = 'bundled-fonts')]. The feature is OFF by default in MT-003 so the crate compiles without the font file. MT-004 enables the feature when assets are bundled. Add a compile-time note: if 'bundled-fonts' is enabled but the path does not exist, the include_bytes! macro will fail at compile time with a clear message.

wp_validator_review.reviewer: INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR(acting WP_VALIDATOR) session boa3epvo9 — operator-directed retro WP-Validator pass
wp_validator_review.method: deep code review + adversarial verify vs MT/WP/Master-Spec intent (governance paperwork non-blocking per operator)
wp_validator_review.one_line: Independent re-verification confirms the first reviewer: the theme token system honestly and correctly implements the MT-003 contract with verbatim-ported dark/light palettes, a muted syntax set, graceful override/hex/rgba parsing, a REAL runtime-switch toggle (interactive egui widget, stable Id->NodeId 10, Role::Button via widget_info + Sense::click) proven end-to-end via kittest against the real HandshakeApp, and a non-vacuous no-hardcoded-Color32 invariant that I re-ran to zero matches across all 30 MTs; the rgba (6,36,17,46) deviation is egui-correct arithmetic I recomputed, not a fudge.

acceptance_summary.criterion: test_theme passes: all palette token value tests (each Color32 field vs expected hex)
acceptance_summary.evidence: tests/test_theme.rs light_palette_tokens_match_react_root (26-41) and dark_palette_tokens_match_react_dark (45-58) assert every field; palette.rs light()/dark() (93-160) use the exact ported hex. Ground truth: curated suite green.
syntax.rs:28 keyword=from_rgb(0x56,0x9c,0xd6); test_theme.rs:142 asserts it. punctuation=text_subtle (syntax.rs:33), background=bg (syntax.rs:34) derived as the contract specified.
palette.rs with_overrides (169-199) matches 'bg' key; test_theme.rs override_applies_known_hex_key (176-183) asserts red and that accent (non-overridden) is unchanged.

acceptance_summary.criterion: Unknown override key silently ignored, no panic, palette otherwise unchanged
acceptance_summary.evidence: with_overrides _ => {} arm (195) plus parse_color None => continue (171-173); test_theme.rs override_unknown_key_is_ignored (186-190) and override_invalid_hex_is_ignored (193-198) assert whole-struct equality to dark(). HsPalette derives PartialEq/Eq (palette.rs:48).

acceptance_summary.criterion: apply_to_ctx does not panic on headless ctx with light or dark
acceptance_summary.evidence: egui_apply.rs apply_to_ctx (19-37) maps every contract-listed Visuals/Widgets field; test_theme.rs apply_to_ctx_does_not_panic_and_sets_panel_fill (216-233) runs both palettes on egui::Context::default() and asserts panel_fill reaches the ctx for each.

acceptance_summary.criterion: Running app + theme toggle switches window bg dark<->light (screenshot/kittest)
acceptance_summary.evidence: test_theme.rs app_toggle_switches_panel_fill_dark_to_light (237-267) builds the REAL HandshakeApp via with_health, drives app.ui(ctx) through egui_kittest::Harness, asserts initial panel_fill==dark.bg and current_theme()==Dark, clicks get_by_label("Light"), reruns, asserts panel_fill==light.bg and current_theme()==Light. NOT #[ignore]'d (no attribute above the #[test]); end-to-end through the real toggle->current_theme->apply_theme_if_changed (app.rs:3712-3716, 3659-3668, 3906) path, not tautological.

acceptance_summary.criterion: Theme toggle has AccessKit NodeId(10) + Role::Button
acceptance_summary.evidence: app.rs theme_toggle (3675-3717) builds a real interactive widget: Id::from_high_entropy_bits(THEME_TOGGLE_NODE_ID=10) (app.rs:38,3683), ui.interact(Sense::click) (3690), widget_info(WidgetType::Button) (3701-3703), stable author_id via accessibility::emit_interactive_node (3710; live.rs:128-132 sets author_id while preserving egui's derived Role::Button+Action::Click). test_accesskit_ids.rs live_tree_contains_theme_toggle_by_author_id (260-289) asserts role=="Button" on the LIVE tree; line 207-208 reserves 10 for the toggle. Literal numeric NodeId(10) assertion was routed to MT-025 (sibling file), which the first reviewer accepted; behavior is covered.

acceptance_summary.criterion: No widget uses hardcoded Color32 for semantic colors outside palette.rs/syntax.rs (grep test)
acceptance_summary.evidence: test_theme.rs no_hardcoded_color32_outside_theme_module (271-313) recursively scans src/ for from_rgb/WHITE/BLACK excluding palette.rs/syntax.rs. I independently re-ran the exact contract grep over the FINAL 30-MT worktree src tree: zero matches. Invariant is real and held across all later MTs.

acceptance_summary.criterion: cargo check continues to pass (MT-002 skeleton remains compilable)
acceptance_summary.evidence: Ground truth (relied upon per directive): whole crate + all test binaries compile clean (cargo build --tests exit 0); curated suite passes 0 failures. lib.rs exposes pub mod theme (39) and pub mod app (7) cleanly.

spec_realism.detail: MT-003 is an in-process token/visuals system. I grepped the theme module + theme-relevant app.rs for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/panic!/'not yet wired'/'deferred to'/'pending MT-': none on the required path. The AccessKit tree and runtime switch are exercised against the REAL HandshakeApp via egui_kittest (test_theme.rs:237-267) and the live AccessKit tree (test_accesskit_ids.rs:260-289), not an implementer-authored mock. The visual-switch proof is NOT #[ignore]'d (runs in the default suite). The theme toggle is a genuine egui interactive widget (ui.interact + Sense::click + widget_info Button), not a fake. The rgba premultiplication deviation (contract's hand-computed (6,35,17,46) -> egui-authoritative (6,36,17,46)) is honestly documented (palette.rs:7-13, test_theme.rs:113-127); I independently recomputed egui's gamma-space fast_round(c*46/255) and confirmed 34->6, 197->36, 94->17 = (6,36,17,46), so the coder fixed a genuine contract arithmetic bug rather than fudging. The backend settings-API binding for theme persistence is explicitly deferred by the contract ('DO NOT persist ... a later MT concern') and the with_overrides()/HsTheme serde forward-compat is real, so no deferred-live escape on any behavior MT-003 must deliver.

concerns_non_blocking: NON-BLOCKER (hygiene): the no-hardcoded-Color32 grep test only matches from_rgb/WHITE/BLACK; it does not catch Color32::from_rgba_unmultiplied/from_gray/named constants. The contract specified exactly those three patterns, so this is within contract; could be tightened in a future hygiene pass. Two Color32::TRANSPARENT no-fill sentinels exist outside the theme module but are structural, not semantic palette colors.

NON-BLOCKER (paperwork): test file lives at tests/test_theme.rs, not the contract's tests/native_gui/test_theme.rs. This is a hard cargo requirement (the --test test_theme target name derives from the file's location in tests/) and is honestly disclosed in the file header (lines 8-12). The acceptance proof command still resolves. Not a code defect.

NON-BLOCKER (paperwork): contract CONTROL-5 said egui pin '=0.31.x'; actual pin is '0.33' (caret, not exact). Justified by the MT-001 spike verdict (rustc 1.91.1 forces 0.33) and documented with the Visuals field list in egui_apply.rs:3-9 for upgrade safety. A future minor could in theory shift Visuals fields; consider an exact '=' pin at installer-hardening time. Low risk.

NON-BLOCKER (context): Cargo.toml sets default=['bundled-fonts'] (font ON by default), whereas the MT-003 CONTROL-6 said OFF-by-default. This is CORRECT in the final tree because MT-004 bundled the Inter font assets (app.rs:649-662 include_bytes! of Inter-Regular.ttf/Inter-Bold.ttf, with a #[cfg(not(feature='bundled-fonts'))] fallback at 3650). Ground truth confirms the crate compiles clean. Not an MT-003 regression — it reflects MT-004 having since run in this folded worktree.

NON-BLOCKER (paperwork): the literal '== NodeId(10)' numeric assertion lives in MT-025's test file (accesskit-stable-ids), not in test_theme.rs. MT-003's own tests prove Role::Button + stable author_id + working toggle on the live tree; the numeric reservation is asserted at test_accesskit_ids.rs:208. Behavior fully covered.

CERTIFICATION NOTE: this WP was built folded with no separate validator by design; this adversarial review IS the independent certification (Spec-Realism sub-rule 3 satisfied). Implementer self-certification / lifecycle paperwork state is not a FAIL reason per operator directive.
